operation: Root Cause Analysis - TypeScript Compilation Failure

root_cause_identified.issue: Registry 'name' field used for TypeScript identifiers
root_cause_identified.example: project-screen-1 has name='Project Screen 1 (TBD)' → Generated Type='Project Screen 1 (TBD)Record'
root_cause_identified.impact: Spaces and parentheses are invalid in TypeScript type names

root_cause_identified.issue: entity_type field empty in registry for project screens
PascalCase applied to component filenames, but entity types still invalid
root_cause_identified.impact: Component names PascalCased, but types still use registry display names

solution_strategy.approach: Use LayerId-based naming instead of registry 'name' field
steps: 1. Sanitize layer naming: Use LayerId or ToPascalCase(LayerId) for code identifiers
2. Do NOT use registry.name field for component/type names (only for display/comments)
4. Preserve registry.name for documentation only (file comments, type annotations)
5. Regenerate all 173 screens with corrected generation logic

code_fixes_needed.line_219_fixed: # DO NOT use registry name for code identifiers - it has spaces/parentheses

estimated_scope.estimated_fixes: 50,755 type errors should reduce to ~100 (edge cases)

dpdca_v2_decision.checkpoint: Can we proceed to ACT phase?
dpdca_v2_decision.blocker: Entity types in generated TypeScript are invalid - must fix and regenerate
dpdca_v2_decision.next_phase: Implement code fixes, regenerate, re-validate TypeScript
